pending.body: Stop swallowing partial lease-cleanup failures in the single-repo daemon loop. `release_task_lease()` now reports `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure` when worktree/lease cleanup only partially succeeds (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3640-3686`), but `process_cycle()` still catches every `process_task()` error, logs it, and continues (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1127-1134`). In single-repo mode `run()` therefore keeps returning success and dispatching later tasks after a terminal cleanup left contradictory durable state (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:235-260`). Bubble this error out of `process_cycle()` or otherwise stop/quarantine the daemon when lease cleanup is partial.

Wire the daemon cancellation-handoff journal repair into the shared status/resume path instead of keeping it daemon-internal. The new repair helper only runs via `load_dispatch_run_snapshot()`, and that loader is only called right before daemon dispatch (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2554-2564`, `2647-2655`, `2908-2921`). `run status`/terminal sync and `run resume` still call the old signal-only repair helper (`src/cli/run.rs:1972-2024`, `1096-1108`, `3212-3217`), whose summary check does not recognize the daemon cancellation summaries. If the daemon dies in the handoff window, those user-facing recovery flows still leave the journal missing its terminal `run_failed` event.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 5 -> Plan and Implement
